mainText:
### Colombia Picks Its Populist

President Donald Trump has endorsed both candidates in the South Carolina Republican gubernatorial runoff, which effectively diminishes the endorsement's significance, likening it to a participation trophy.

#### British Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns
Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced outside Downing Street that he will resign less than two years after winning a landslide victory. A new Labour leader and prime minister will be in place by September, with nominations opening on July 9. Starmer confirmed he would remain in office until the contest concludes to ensure an orderly transition. He did not endorse or mention Andy Burnham, the likely successor, whose recent by-election win prompted Starmer's resignation. If Burnham wins the leadership contest, he would become the sixth prime minister in seven years. Nigel Farage, leader of the right-wing anti-immigration Reform UK party, demanded an immediate general election, which is unlikely.

- Starmer entered office with a strong parliamentary majority but lost popularity due to cuts to winter fuel payments, an inheritance tax on family farms, and his ambassador's ties to Jeffrey Epstein.
- Under his leadership, waiting lists at the National Health Service fell to their lowest in three and a half years, net migration halved, and the asylum backlog reached its lowest since 2020. He claimed success in ridding the Labour Party of antisemitism.
- Larry the cat, Downing Street's Chief Mouser since 2011, has outlasted six prime ministers.

#### Next Stage of Iran Talks Begins
Vice President J.D. Vance met with Iranian officials in Switzerland for diplomatic talks, marking the start of a 60-day negotiation period concerning Tehran’s nuclear program. Iran had previously claimed to have resealed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli operations against Hezbollah, but U.S. officials denied any military action against merchant shipping occurred. U.S. Central Command reported that over 55 merchant ships carrying more than 17 million barrels of oil transited the strait over the weekend. Iranian officials had delayed talks but eventually agreed to a ceasefire with Hezbollah.

- As Vance met with Iranian officials, Trump claimed he would strike Iran “very hard again” if it fails to control Hezbollah.
- Israel and Lebanese officials are expected to meet in Washington for peace negotiations this week.

#### Spanish Prime Minister’s Wife To Stand Trial
Judge Juan Carlos Peinado ordered Begoña Gómez, wife of Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, to stand trial for misusing public funds and leveraging personal relationships to advance her career. She must surrender her passport and report to court twice monthly. The trial date is yet to be set, with charges stemming from a broader investigation into corruption within Sánchez’s government. Sánchez has not been accused of wrongdoing and dismissed the charges as politically motivated.

- Previously, Sánchez's brother stood trial for patronage job accusations, and former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero is under investigation for influence peddling and money laundering.
- The main opposition party, the Partido Popular, has called for Sánchez to resign and for early elections.

#### Cuba To Start Opening Economy
Cuban Prime Minister Manuel Marrero Cruz announced that the country will begin opening critical economic sectors to foreign investment amid severe shortages and public discontent. This shift includes privatizing state companies and a package of 176 measures that allows for currency devaluation and relaxes state control over key industries.

- In May, Trump imposed targeted sanctions on Cuba, tightening the embargo that has been in place since 1962.
- Trump has also indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro and expressed willingness to intervene militarily against Cuba.

#### Charles Schwab To Add Prediction Markets
Charles Schwab is partnering with Cboe Global Markets to allow customers to wager on the performance of the S&P; 500, offering binary options on whether the index will close above or below a target price. This move marks a significant shift as mainstream financial institutions begin to embrace prediction markets.
